name: The Gate
description: A worn stone archway marks the edge of the market. The stones are smooth at shoulder height where generations of arrivals have steadied themselves before going in. Through the arch, the noise of the market begins.

scenery.description: The arch was built by somebody who expected it to outlast the market. So far it is winning.
scenery.rejection: The arch is load-bearing and intends to stay that way.

name: Market Square, South End
description: The quieter end of the square, where foot traffic from the gate sorts itself into currents. A notice board leans under the weight of its postings. Paths lead off toward food smells, lens-glint, and the main press of the market to the north.

name: Market Square
description: The full press of the market. Awnings of mismatched fabric filter the light into patches of color over a salt vendor's stall and a glassblower's stand, with more stalls wedged between them than the square has corners. Someone is trying to deliver a crate to a stall that has moved since yesterday.

scenery.description: No two awnings match. They are patched with material from other awnings, which were themselves patched. Somewhere at the bottom of the stack there may be an original awning, the way there is an original fire.
scenery.rejection: The awnings are tied off well above your reach.

scenery.description: The crate is addressed in four scripts. Three of them disagree about the destination, and the fourth might be a recipe.
scenery.rejection: The courier has strong opinions about people touching the crate.

name: Rawstock Row
description: A narrow lane of raw materials: bins of powders, coils of wire, jars of liquids in colors that are making a fair argument against existing. The vendors are specialists — they know what they're selling even if you don't. To the east, the lane funnels into something tighter.

scenery.description: The bins are labeled by smell, apparently. The labels are blank, and the vendors sniff before quoting a price.
scenery.rejection: The vendors watch the bins the way some people watch their children.

name: Tiv's Materials
description: A stall with serious shelving. Tiv stocks materials for people who make things: dusts, wires, clays, shards, each in its place and priced. The good stock is at eye level and the better stock is not.

name: The Open Forge
description: A working forge open to the lane on two sides. Tools hang in size order over the bench — hammer, tongs, a mortar that has ground stranger things than grain. The forge itself breathes heat in slow pulses, and a sign lists the rules, which everyone follows except the ones they don't.

scenery.description: The forge inhales when Grist pumps the bellows and exhales when they don't. It is not strictly clear which of them is in charge of the rhythm.
scenery.rejection: You could touch the heat, once.

name: Food Row
description: A stretch of the market given over to food. Steam rises from a griddle stand where something flat and round is frying, and a vat of broth stands tall enough to need a ladder. The smells change every few steps and at least two of them are arguing. A bench along the wall holds vendors on their breaks, eating quickly.

scenery.description: The broth has been going for longer than the stall has existed. The vendor describes it as 'mature.' The ladder is for stirring, allegedly.
scenery.rejection: The broth vendor stands between you and the vat with the calm of someone who has done this before.

scenery.description: Vendors eat here on their breaks, fast and silent. Professional courtesy: nobody reviews the food.
scenery.rejection: The bench is fully occupied by people with strong elbows.

name: The Nut Cart
description: A cart at the corner of the square, warm to stand near. Pell roasts nuts in a drum that turns by some arrangement nobody asks about, and sells them by the paper cone.

name: Wire & Filament
description: A stall hung with coils: copper, silver, and stranger threads that catch the light wrong. Spool measures lengths by arm-span and rounds in the customer's favor, mostly.

name: The Lens Grinder's Bench
description: A workbench dusted with glass so fine it hangs in the light. Vero grinds lenses with a foot-treadle and a patience that predates the treadle. Finished work sits in a tray, looking back.

name: Oddments Alley
description: A crooked alley between the market proper and the quieter commerce beyond. The Collector keeps a table here of things that are each the last of something. East, through a gap, there's a courtyard nobody uses.

name: The Fair Exchange
description: The quiet heart of the stranger market. Stalls here post terms instead of prices — three hours of your afternoon, a carried message, the loan of a skill. A price board lists today's rates of exchange between things that should not have an exchange rate. Everyone reads the fine print, because here it is the large print too.

scenery.description: Each posted term is precise: 'one genuine apology, delivered standing.' 'The memory of a meal, donor's choice.' The vendors will not explain what the terms are FOR. That information costs extra.
scenery.rejection: The terms are posted behind glass, which is either protective or load-bearing.

scenery.description: The fine print, examined closely, is composed of smaller print. You stop before finding out if it continues.
scenery.rejection: The print declines to be touched. This is in the fine print.

name: The Broker's Nook
description: A recessed shopfront, dim and orderly. Madame Wick brokers deals between parties who prefer not to meet, weighing offers on a brass scale that measures more than weight.

name: The Bindery
description: A narrow shop smelling of paste and old paper. Quill binds books, contracts, and the occasional agreement that needs to be physically restrained. Loose pages are weighted down with whatever was nearest.

name: Rag Court
description: A small courtyard off the alley, paved and empty. Lines are strung overhead for laundry or banners; today they hold neither.

name: The Rendering Yards
description: A wide threshold where the market's noise gives way to the Yards' deeper racket. Carts pass through loaded one way and unloaded the other.

name: The Smelting Bay
description: Heat, noise, and the smell of metals changing their minds. Kelp works the crucibles, pouring off slag that cools in colors slag shouldn't have. Visitors are tolerated at the yellow line.

name: The Press House
description: A shed built around a press the size of a smaller shed. Drum feeds it material and it returns sheets thin enough to read through. The floor shakes on the downstroke, which is how everyone here tells time.

name: The Cinder Path
description: A service path of packed ash between the Yards and the forge lane. It exists so the carts don't have to cross the square, and it looks like it.

name: The Back Lane
description: A lane behind Food Row, used mostly by people leaving. The market sounds thin out with every step west.

name: The Old Stalls
description: A row of abandoned stall frames, bare as winter trees. Whatever market stood here moved on and took the noise with it. Dust has the counters now.

scenery.description: The frames are sound, under the dust. Somebody built them to last and then the lasting outlived the point.
scenery.rejection: The frames creak a warning when leaned on.

name: Canvas Rot
description: Further in, the old awnings still hang, rotted to streamers that move without much wind. It is very quiet.

name: The Squatter's Corner
description: One stall at the end of the row has been swept, patched, and lived in. A bedroll, a crate-table, a tin kettle — someone keeps this corner against the emptiness, and is not home.

name: The Crush
description: The market's overflow, compressed into a lane built for half of it. Stalls stand on stalls; wares hang overhead because up was the only direction left. Two vendors are selling out of the same counter in shifts, and the shift is changing. You move when the lane moves.

scenery.description: The hanging wares are arranged by weight, heaviest lowest, which says something reassuring about the knots. Tags dangle at eye level. Half of the prices are crossed out and revised upward.
scenery.rejection: Touch a hanging ware and three vendors materialize to discuss your intentions.

scenery.description: The shared counter has a chalk line down the middle, redrawn so often the chalk has worn a groove. The current line is under dispute. The dispute has a waiting list.
scenery.rejection: There is no room at the counter. There is barely room at the dispute.

name: The Crush, Deeper
description: The lane narrows until commerce becomes single-file. The stalls down here sell to each other as much as to anyone, goods circulating in a tight orbit. A lantern burns overhead at noon because the wares have closed over the sky. The far end is not visible and possibly not there.

scenery.description: The lantern is communal, refilled by whoever notices it dimming. A small plaque credits its founding donor, whose name has been respectfully worn away by thumbs.
scenery.rejection: The lantern hangs from a beam that itself hangs from an arrangement.
